# ++CHRCH Access

*Section: THE BOOK OF DARES: Life Is Art*

- id: 12114744
- url: https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/12114744

## videos

- `iframe` https://dialog.filestackapi.com/dialog/comm_iframe/
- `iframe` https://www.filestackapi.com/dialog/comm_iframe/
- `iframe` https://js.stripe.com/v3/controller-with-preconnect-5923ab14c9a2faa0ad2f0f95c16ea979.html#__shared_params__[version]=v3&__shared_params__[light_experiment_assignments]=%7B%22token%22%3A%2217ce40c1-e66e-4423-94b5-d7436ec42def%22%2C%22assignments%22%3A%7B%22link_ewcs_prewarm_experiment_v2%22%3A%22treatment%22%7D%7D&apiKey=pk_live_hkgvgSBxG4TAl3zGlXiB1KUX&stripeJsId=17ce40c1-e66e-4423-94b5-d7436ec42def&stripeObjId=sobj-2321b4bd-474c-481e-b894-322630a5bdce&firstStripeInstanceCreatedLatency=1966&controllerCount=1&isCheckout=false&stripeJsLoadTime=1778071987555&manualBrowserDeprecationRollout=false&mids[guid]=NA&mids[muid]=53e126fd-04c3-48c6-890f-b04f0b26f45c352beb&mids[sid]=fdbd9602-a5fb-4729-889c-fc318f4a977cddf210&referrer=https%3A%2F%2Flearn.makeartnotcontent.com%2Fcourses%2F465756%2Flectures%2F12114744&controllerId=__privateStripeController2841
- `iframe` https://js.stripe.com/v3/m-outer-3437aaddcdf6922d623e172c2d6f9278.html#url=https%3A%2F%2Flearn.makeartnotcontent.com%2Fcourses%2F465756%2Flectures%2F12114744&title=11.%20How%20I%20Ended%20Up%20Getting%20Those%20Epic%20Ashley%20Olsen%20Photos%20%7C%20The&referrer=&muid=53e126fd-04c3-48c6-890f-b04f0b26f45c352beb&sid=fdbd9602-a5fb-4729-889c-fc318f4a977cddf210&version=6&preview=false&__shared_params__[version]=v3

---

##    11\. How I Ended Up Getting Those Epic Ashley Olsen Photos 

> **"The best photos are of normal people doing extraordinary things, or famous people doing normal things." - Andy Warhol**

I love that Warhol quote.

Even US Weekly created that "Just Like Us" column that is "crack" to females waiting for their flights at airports.

But my favorite photos to take? Besides making regular people look like comets in the sky?

_Is of famous people doing unusual things._

I mean, why get the same photo that everyone else is getting, right?

That's why most of the time you'll see me flat-out ignore all the celebrities in the room. Most of the time they're too afraid of being "not on brand". At least the B-celebrities are. In the time that it takes to deal with their publicist, I could have taken 10 more awesome pictures. And the funniest part of it all? Being "not on brand" is 10 times better for their career than being "on brand" ever was. But I digress...

**So, while every other photographer is trying to get their "money shot" in the V.I.P. section... They leave the whole rest of the room, all to me, to discover an "unknown superstar", being totally ignored**

That's why often photographers will say to me the next day "When I see your photos, it's like we photographed two different parties."

And that's exactly the energy I want to bring to parties when I go out.

PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #10: Ignore old celebrities. Create new ones.

But once in a while, a friend will introduce me to someone who happens to be famous.

Like Marvin, the founder of Nylon Magazine did that night.

But you have understand my frame of mind leading up to this encounter... 

I only cared about one thing:

_Who will be my triptych tonight?_

Let me explain.

Back then, every night I would find someone to be my "triptych pinup". For the non-technical: a triptych is when 3 photos are presented as one.

I don't usually care for those "artsy" photographer concepts. But my "triptych pinups" developed out of pure necessity. 

You see, back then, I never took vertical photos. I had a very 'IKEA' attitude about my online galleries. Everything had to be in a grid. 

But that meant that I couldn't get whole outfits unless I was really far away.

Or...

I made the person lie down somewhere.

Which I would do, but that got boring to me.

But, through [the process of iterations](https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/11925540), I developed this new thing... Photograph them in 3 separate sections. Then stitch the photos together!

Just being creative... You know?

So when I met Ashley Olsen... That's what I told her I wanted to do with her.

First words out of my mouth.

Of course as soon as she lay down everyone was freaking out like "What's going on?".

_So imagine this. While Ashley Olsen is lying there... The crowd of partiers back away to make room for us... And to get a better view of this unusual scene unfolding... Whispering... Tapping each others shoulders..._

Which I didn't mind but one problem... After I took the first test photo, I realized the photo looked "empty". 

**So I had to tell the whole crowd to COME BACK. Move CLOSER. I needed them to crowd us. Get around the couch so that I could see the feet all around her. I needed people who would see this photo to understand that it was taken at a PARTY.**

Reluctantly, people did it. All it took was some of my trusted muses scattered in the room to move first. They never needed "the memo". And Ashley patiently waited smiling.

"Closer... Ok. YOU. Closer..."

And then... When I started by taking photos of Ashley Olsen's feet everyone just wondered "What the hell is going ON?"

_Well this is what the hell was going on:_

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/99nl37F7SGW3QlAgZgwl_ashleyOlson.png)

All of this to tell you...

Don't be afraid to get the legendary photo.

Don't be polite just because it's a celebrity.

If anyone understands the power of an image... It's them. At least the A-celebrities do.

So always, always GO FOR IT.

_And then later you can ask to take the "boring photo" that the media outlets will demand for "coverage" the next day._

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/VW2DZVAtQryEK5eNcNuK_IMG_2227x.jpg)

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/12114744/comments/4417769)

Just dawned on me that the real magic in all of this is your story telling ability. A message is nothing with out its a delivery and the delivery is always best through story. I think for many it may be possible to find a message but to deliver it concisely through story for max impact could be difficult. Could be an interesting lesson in itself - unless of course im overlooking a lesson you've already done on this. Also, What did Ashley say after you to the pics? Did she see them?

very very perceptive... you're right, there are multiple levels BUT none is more important than the base: your message. This is the context that will give your stories their poignancy. Since I don't look at photos at the party, I never got her firsthand reaction. But my friend at the magazine told me she loved the pin-up.
